reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (2/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output document is missing a massive volume of the original content. Specifically, Page 4, Pages 7 through 12, and Pages 14 through 48 are entirely absent from the conversion. The output JSON explicitly contains a metadata property acknowledging that 42 sections were omitted, which represents a critical loss of content for a 61-page document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source document contains 61 pages of text, including full text for Section A (items 3-11 on Page 4), Sections B, C, D, E, F, and G.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON sections contain gaps in reading_order (jumps from 3 to 5, 6 to 13, and 13 to 49) and includes the string: '"_omitted": "42 sections omitted for token budget"'.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, missing reading_order indexes and page content.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Substantial content is missing within sections that are partially present in the output. Section A (Intent, Use, Duration, and Scope) only includes items 1 and 2, while items 3 through 11 (a full page of text) are omitted. Section C (Specifications) is missing points 1-15 and everything from point 20 onwards. These are major omissions within the logical structure of the document.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Section A (Intent, Use, Duration, and Scope) items 3 through 11 on page 4; Section C (Specifications) items 1 through 15 on pages 9 and 10.
concerns.output_quote: Section A element ends abruptly at item 2 (reading_order 29); Section C elements only cover items 16-19 (reading_orders 131-137).

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is inconsistent across major document sections. Major sections (the Attachments) toggle between H1 and H2 levels without a logical structural reason. For example, Attachment A is assigned H1, while Attachments B, C, D, and E are H2, and then Attachments F and G return to H1. Top-level sections should consistently use the same heading level.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Major sections like Attachment A, Attachment B, Attachment F, and Attachment G are logically at the same hierarchical level in the document.
concerns.output_quote: Attachment A (H1, reading_order 505), Attachment B (H2, reading_order 510), Attachment F (H1, reading_order 563), Attachment G (H1, reading_order 568).
concerns.location: Throughout the document, specifically pages 49, 50, 59, and 60.

concerns.analysis: Table D.1 on page 56 is structurally corrupted. In the source, the table has three columns: 'IT Enterprise Hardware Segment', 'IT Enterprise Hardware Sub-Segment', and 'Minimum Percentage Discount Off MSRP'. The 'Servers' segment spans three rows in the first column. In the output, the sub-segment names ('Software', 'Extended Warranties') from the second column have been shifted into the first column, and the second column is left empty. concerns.source_pdf_quote: Table D.1: Row 2 contains sub-segment 'Software' in the second column and part of the 'Servers' segment.
concerns.output_quote: Row 2: cells[0]="Software", cells[1]="", Row 3: cells[0]="Extended Warranties", cells[1]=""

concerns.analysis: Several nested lists throughout the document have not been converted into structured list elements. Instead, they are rendered as plain text inside a paragraph block with newline characters (`\n`) used to separate items. This loses the structural meaning of the list.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 6, Item 8: 'a. “This offer does not constitute a binding offer”, b. “This offer will be valid only if this offer is selected as a finalist or in the competitive range”, ...'
concerns.output_quote: "text": "8. **NON-RESPONSIVE OFFERS:** ... \na. \u201cThis offer does not constitute a binding offer\u201d,\nb. \u201cThis offer will be valid only if this offer is selected as a finalist or in the competitive range\u201d, ..."
concerns.location: Section B, Item 8, Page 6, element index (reading_order) 58
